claimText: The Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services (INCOIS) is an autonomous body established under the Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES).
evidenceExcerpt: Established as an autonomous body in 1999 under Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES).
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the nodal ministry and nature of the organization for INCOIS.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
Statutory body instead of autonomous body

claimText: INCOIS operates as a unit of the Earth System Science Organization (ESSO).
evidenceExcerpt: Unit of the Earth System Science Organization (ESSO).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the organizational structure and parent organizations of INCOIS.
distractorHooks: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR)

claimText: INCOIS provides services such as the Potential Fishing Zone (PFZ) Advisory, Tsunami Early Warning System, Ocean State Forecast, and Coral Bleaching Alerts System.
evidenceExcerpt: Other INCOIS Services: Potential Fishing Zone (PFZ) Advisory, Tsunami Early Warning System, Ocean State Forecast (OSF), Coral Bleaching Alerts System etc.
examUse: Multiple-statement question asking which of the given early warning or advisory services are provided by INCOIS.
distractorHooks: Cyclone naming and tracking (primarily done by IMD)
Earthquake monitoring (done by National Centre for Seismology)
Forest fire alerts (done by Forest Survey of India)
